calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-26-2011 Off Calendar

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-26-2011 Off Calendar

Petition For Declaration That First Amendment To Revocable Trust Of Robert J Foppiano Is Void, For Damages For Elder Abuse, And For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Mar-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Hugo Torbet for James Foppiano. Don Dowling for Joan Foppiano.

The matter was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial, (order for the same signed in open court).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Declaration That First Amendment To Revocable Trust Of Robert J Foppiano Is Void, For Damages For Elder Abuse, And For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Jan-12-2011 Continued To Mar-16-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Don Dowling for Joan Fappiano (Respondnet). Hugo Torbet for Petitioner.

Mr. Torbet reqeusted a 90-day continuance stating that he was in the middle of discovery, that he needed to take a couple to depositions and that he needed to subpeona medical records. Mr. Dowling stated that he was done with discovery, that mediation would be a waste of time and offered to have his Client authorize release of medical records. The matter was continued to March 16, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of trial setting. The Court stated that it was not inclined to enter default against the two Respondents that have not responded as requested by Mr. Torbet.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Declaration That First Amendment To Revocable Trust Of Robert J Foppiano Is Void, For Damages For Elder Abuse, And For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Nov-02-2010 Continued To Jan-12-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Hugo Torbet for James Foppiano.

Mr. Torbet stated that they had exchanged one round of written discovery, that there has been one deposition, and that what comes next will be a subpoena for medical records and a couple more depositions. The Court continued the matter to January 12, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on discovery and for trial setting.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Court Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Petition For Declaration That First Amendment To Revocable Trust Of Robert J Foppiano Is Void, For Damages For Elder Abuse, And For Removal Of Trustee
Off Calendar Jun-29-2010 Continued To Nov-02-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Don Dowling for Joan Foppiano (Respondent). Hugo Torbet for James Foppiano.

The Court granted the waiver of notice to Margaret Foppiano. Mr. Torbet stated that discovery needed to be conducted and that the matter may be ready for trial in 6 months. The matter was continued to November 2, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and for trial setting. A written status report is due by October 28, 2010.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
